text.title: Usability Testing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Usability Testing. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Tasks are framed as user goals ('book a flight to Lisbon'), not feature drills ('use the date picker'); feature drills bias toward existing interactions.

rationale.source: Steve Krug — Rocket Surgery Made Easy

rule.rationale: Feature drills miss the path users would actually take.

rule.statement: Participants think aloud; moderators avoid leading questions and only probe after silence.

rationale.source: Nielsen — Why You Only Need to Test with 5 Users

rule.rationale: Think-aloud surfaces mental model gaps that observation alone cannot.

rule.statement: At least five representative users per persona segment are tested; more than nine has diminishing returns for a single design version.

rationale.source: Nielsen — Why You Only Need to Test with 5 Users

rule.rationale: Below five misses 50 percent of issues; beyond nine adds noise per cost.

rule.statement: Every finding gets a 1-to-4 severity rating tied to task-completion impact, not aesthetic preference.

rationale.source: Nielsen — Severity Ratings for Usability Problems

rule.rationale: Without severity ratings, every finding looks equally urgent.

rule.statement: Recordings and transcripts redact personally identifiable data before sharing outside the research team.
rule.rationale: Sharing raw PII breaches research ethics and law.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-usability-testing.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
